tools.name: classify_vulnerability
tools.description: Classify a vulnerability description into severity level (low/medium/high/critical) using CIRCL's VLAI RoBERTa model. Returns severity label, confidence score, and all class probabilities.

tools.name: clear_memories
tools.description: Admin: delete all memories. Requires confirm_token='YES_DELETE_ALL'. Cannot be undone.

tools.name: clone_voice
tools.description: Clone a voice from reference audio and speak text with it. Requires fish-speech to be installed.

tools.name: convert_cad
tools.description: Convert between CAD/mesh formats (STL/3MF/OBJ/PLY; STEP read is best-effort if build123d/OCP is present). Returns the output path/URL.

tools.name: convert_document
tools.description: Convert between document formats using pandoc

tools.name: create_excel
tools.description: Create an Excel spreadsheet

tools.name: create_powerpoint
tools.description: Create a PowerPoint presentation

tools.name: create_word_document
tools.description: Create a Word document

tools.name: execute_bash
tools.description: Execute Bash commands in an isolated Docker container

tools.name: execute_nodejs
tools.description: Execute Node.js code in an isolated Docker container

tools.name: execute_powershell
tools.description: Execute a PowerShell script in an isolated Docker container (pwsh on Alpine)

tools.name: execute_python
tools.description: Execute Python code in an isolated Docker container

tools.name: explore_repository
tools.description: Locate relevant code via the FastContext-4B explorer subagent. Returns file+line citations. Call before editing.

tools.name: forget
tools.description: Delete a specific memory by ID. Use when recall returns a stale or incorrect memory.

tools.name: get_loaded_models
tools.description: Return which Ollama models are currently loaded in VRAM/RAM with size and expiry.

tools.name: get_metrics_summary
tools.description: Return key Portal 5 operational metrics from Prometheus (requests, tool calls, errors, TPS).

tools.name: get_pipeline_status
tools.description: Return Portal 5 pipeline health: backend count, workspace count, version.

tools.name: get_workspace_recommendation
tools.description: Suggest the best Portal 5 workspace for a plain-English task description.

tools.name: kb_ingest
tools.description: Admin: ingest files from a directory into a knowledge base. Reads .md, .txt, .pdf, .docx, .pptx, .xlsx, .html, .htm, .epub files (Docling-first extraction with pypdf/python-docx fallback). Run via curl or as setup; not typically called from chat.

tools.name: kb_list
tools.description: List all available knowledge bases (KBs) and their document counts.

tools.name: kb_optimize
tools.description: Admin: build an IVF_PQ vector index on a KB for faster search. Skipped automatically for KBs under 256 chunks (brute-force is already fast). Run after large ingests.

tools.name: kb_restore
tools.description: Admin: restore a KB to an earlier LanceDB version (see kb_versions). The restore itself is a new version, so it can be undone.

tools.name: kb_search
tools.description: Search a specific knowledge base. Returns top relevant chunks with source file and similarity score. Use kb_list first to find available KB IDs. query_type: vector (semantic, default), fts (BM25 keyword — exact terms/IDs), hybrid (both, RRF-fused). fts/hybrid require the KB to be ingested with fts=true.

tools.name: kb_search_all
tools.description: Search across all knowledge bases simultaneously. Useful when the user's question may match multiple KBs. query_type: vector (default), fts, hybrid; KBs without an FTS index transparently fall back to vector.

tools.name: kb_versions
tools.description: List a KB's LanceDB version history and named tags (e.g. automatic pre-rebuild tags). Use with kb_restore to roll back.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List files and directories at the given path. Returns [FILE]/[DIR] prefixed entries.

tools.name: list_generated_files
tools.description: List recently generated documents in the output directory. Use this to find files created by other tools.

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: List stored memories, optionally filtered by category or tag. For inventory and management.

tools.name: list_voices
tools.description: List available voices for text-to-speech.

tools.name: list_workspaces
tools.description: List all Portal 5 workspaces (AI models) with routing metadata. Optional substring filter.

tools.name: mitre_data_sources_for_technique
tools.description: List data sources needed to detect a technique.

tools.name: mitre_detections_for_technique
tools.description: List local SPL detections for a technique (joins ATT&CK to our detection library).

tools.name: mitre_technique_lookup
tools.description: Look up a MITRE ATT&CK technique by ID. Returns name, tactic, platforms, detection info.

tools.name: mitre_techniques_list
tools.description: List all known ATT&CK techniques with their metadata.

tools.name: news_search
tools.description: Search recent news articles. Biased toward news sources and recent results.

tools.name: read_excel
tools.description: Extract data from an existing Excel (.xlsx) spreadsheet. Returns sheet names and row data for each sheet.

tools.name: read_pdf
tools.description: Extract text content from an existing PDF file page by page. Also extracts tables when present. Requires pdfplumber.

tools.name: read_powerpoint
tools.description: Extract text and speaker notes from an existing PowerPoint (.pptx) file. Returns slide titles, content, and notes.

tools.name: read_text_file
tools.description: Read a file from the host filesystem. Accepts absolute paths or repo-relative paths. Returns line-numbered content.

tools.name: read_word_document
tools.description: Extract text content and structure from an existing Word (.docx) file. Returns headings, paragraphs, and table data.

tools.name: recall
tools.description: Retrieve memories relevant to a query. Returns top matches by semantic similarity with recency boost. Use at the start of a conversation to prime context.

tools.name: remember
tools.description: Store a memory for future recall. Use for: user preferences, persistent facts about the user's projects/work, important conclusions to keep across conversations. Each memory should be self-contained — no pronouns referring to the current chat context.

tools.name: render_mesh
tools.description: Render a 3D mesh file (STL/3MF/OBJ/PLY) to a PNG image. Headless, no GUI. Returns the PNG path/URL and the mesh bounding-box dimensions in model units.

tools.name: render_openscad
tools.description: Render OpenSCAD source code to a PNG using the openscad binary in headless --render mode.

tools.name: sandbox_status
tools.description: Check if the code sandbox (Docker/DinD) is available

tools.name: search_files
tools.description: Search for a regex pattern across project files. Returns file:line matches.

tools.name: speak
tools.description: Convert text to speech. Returns path to generated audio file.

tools.name: spl_diff_hypothesis
tools.description: Compare an SPL result against an expected signal to find matches, misses, and unexpected findings.

tools.name: spl_explain_detection
tools.description: Explain a detection: logic, mappings, expected signal.

tools.name: spl_search_library
tools.description: Search the SPL detection library by keyword or technique ID.

tools.name: spl_techniques_covered
tools.description: List all technique IDs with SPL detections.

tools.name: spl_validate_syntax
tools.description: Validate SPL syntax locally (no live Splunk call needed).

tools.name: transcribe_audio
tools.description: Transcribe audio using faster-whisper. If the user uploaded an audio file, omit audio_path and the tool will auto-detect the most recently uploaded file from the workspace.

tools.name: transcribe_with_speakers
tools.description: Transcribe an audio file with speaker diarization (Docker fallback path). On Apple Silicon, prefer the host-native MLX path on port 8924 (mlx-whisper + pyannote on MPS, ~5x faster). This Docker path is the cross-platform fallback for Linux/CUDA nodes.

tools.name: trigger_backend_warmup
tools.description: Pre-load a workspace model into VRAM before a long session.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch the text content of a URL (HTML stripped, max 2MB). Refuses localhost and private addresses.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web. Returns title, URL, snippet for top N results. Use for current events or factual lookups beyond training data.

tools.name: wiki_explain
tools.description: Explain something by searching the canonical layer and returning a cited answer.

tools.name: wiki_get_unit
tools.description: Get a specific knowledge unit by ID with full content and citations.

tools.name: wiki_search
tools.description: Search the canonical knowledge layer by keyword. Returns matching units with citations.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write or overwrite a file on the host filesystem. Constrained to the repo root and /tmp.

scenarios.user_turn: Convert this STL file to 3MF format for my slicer.
Render this 3D mesh file to a PNG image so I can preview it.
Render this OpenSCAD code to a PNG image of the resulting 3D model.
Compare this Splunk query result against the expected signal for T1003.
Explain the logic behind our credential-dumping detection rule.
Find all SPL detections that cover Kerberoasting.
Which technique IDs have SPL detections in our library?
Check if this SPL query has any syntax errors before I run it.
Convert this markdown file to a PDF document.
Create a spreadsheet with the quarterly revenue numbers for each department.
Build a presentation summarizing the key findings from our security audit.
Generate a Word document with the meeting notes from today's standup.
What documents have been generated recently in the output directory?
Extract the data from this Excel spreadsheet so I can analyze it.
Read the contents of this PDF report and extract the key tables.
Pull the text and speaker notes from this PowerPoint presentation.
Extract the text content from this Word document.
Run a shell command to check the disk usage on this machine.
Execute a quick JavaScript snippet to parse some JSON data.
Run a PowerShell command to list running processes on this system.
Run a Python one-liner to calculate the factorial of 20.
Is the code sandbox Docker environment available and healthy?
Delete all stored memories — I want a fresh start.
Remove the memory about my old SSH key configuration.
Show me everything stored in memory right now.
What do you remember about my preferred coding style?
Save that I prefer dark mode and tabs over spaces for indentation.

Look up technique T1190 in the ATT&CK framework.
List all ATT&CK techniques in the initial-access tactic.
Where is the LLM router's workspace detection logic in this codebase?
Which Ollama models are currently loaded in memory right now?
Show me the current request counts and error rates from Prometheus.
Is the pipeline healthy? How many backends and workspaces are configured?
I need to analyze a CVE and write a detection — which workspace should I use?
What files are in the portal/platform/inference/router/ directory?
Show me all available workspaces, especially any related to security.
Read the contents of config/portal.yaml so I can review the workspace definitions.
Find all Python files that reference the ToolRegistry class.
Pre-load the coding workspace model before I start a long session.
Create a new file at /tmp/test_output.txt with the text 'hello world'.
Ingest these markdown files into the security knowledge base.
What knowledge bases are available and how many documents does each have?
Build a vector index on the detection library KB for faster search.
Restore the security KB to the version from before the last bad ingest.
Search the security KB for information about lateral movement techniques.
Search across all knowledge bases for anything related to Cobalt Strike.
Show me the version history of the detection library knowledge base.
Find recent news articles about vulnerabilities in OpenSSH.
Fetch the content of this URL and extract the main text.
Search the web for the latest Ollama release notes.
Classify this CVE description to determine its severity level.
Clone this voice sample and speak the announcement text with it.

Read this paragraph aloud using a female voice.
Transcribe this audio recording to text.

Explain how the tool preselector works in Portal 5.

Fetch the full text content of this specific URL.

Extract text from this Word document.
Store this fact about my preferences for later.
Render this existing STL mesh file to a PNG image.
Explain how the tool preselector works in Portal 5.
List all ATT&CK techniques in the initial-access tactic.
Check if this SPL query has syntax errors.

I found a suspicious binary on a host — analyze the CVE it might exploit and write up a report.
Search for Kerberoasting detections, validate the SPL syntax, then run it in the sandbox.
Read this PDF report, extract the key findings, and save them to a Word document.
What models are loaded, and can you pre-load the coding model before I start?
Search the web for recent proxy shell exploits, then look up the relevant ATT&CK technique.
Transcribe this audio file and store the key points in memory for later.

Create a presentation about our detection coverage — pull stats from the SPL library and format it as slides.
Ingest these documents into the KB, build a vector index, then search for lateral movement.
Check pipeline health, list all workspaces, and tell me which one is best for a security analysis task.
Convert this STL file to 3MF format for my slicer.
Compare this Splunk query result against the expected signal for T1003.
Convert this markdown file to a PDF document.
Run a shell command to check the disk usage on this machine.
Delete all stored memories — I want a fresh start.

Where is the LLM router's workspace detection logic in this codebase?
Ingest these markdown files into the security knowledge base.
Find recent news articles about vulnerabilities in OpenSSH.

What's your opinion on the best programming language for AI?
Tell me a joke about computers.
How are you feeling today?
